name: Arroyo de la Llana River Trail
name_original: Churrón de Borleña por la senda del arroyo La Llana
description: Hike the easy 2.8-mile Arroyo de la Llana River Trail, featuring the Churrón de Borleña Waterfall and Churrón de Castillo.
region_intro: Hiking around Corvera De Toranzo offers access to diverse landscapes within Cantabria, Spain. The region is characterized by lush green mountains, deep valleys, and the Pas River, providing a natural setting for outdoor activities. Hikers can explore areas within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, discover ancient oak forests, and follow paths along riverbanks. The varied terrain includes both gentle riverside walks and more challenging mountain trails. Best hiking trails around Corvera De Toranzo The most popular hiking route is Churrón de Borleña via the La Llana stream path, a 2.8 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 18 minutes to complete. This easy path leads to a spectacular natural waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Conjunto Tumular de Quintana de Toranzo – circular route through the Cajigal de Salcedillo, a moderate 9.5 miles (15.2 km) path. This route explores historical sites and rural landscapes. Local hikers also love the La Piedrona de Villegar – Churrón de Borleña Waterfall loop from Santiurde de Toranzo, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through varied scenery including the waterfall, often completed in about 3 hours 29 minutes. Hiking around Corvera De Toranzo is defined by lush mountains, river valleys, and natural waterfalls. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy family-friendly walks to more demanding treks. The routes in Corvera De Toranzo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Corvera De Toranzo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Corvera De Toranzo?
A: Corvera De Toranzo offers a wide selection of routes, with over 90 hiking trails available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, catering to various experience levels.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in Corvera De Toranzo?
A: Yes, Corvera De Toranzo is well-suited for families and beginners. Approximately 24 of the available routes are classified as easy. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996713975/churron-de-borlena-via-the-la-llana-stream-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña via the La Llana stream path&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 4.4 km trail leading to a spectacular waterfall.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Corvera De Toranzo?
A: The region boasts diverse landscapes, from lush green mountains and deep valleys to the banks of the Pas River. You can explore areas within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, discover ancient oak forests, and enjoy views of traditional rural architecture. The area is known for its natural beauty and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Corvera De Toranzo?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996898593/conjunto-tumular-de-quintana-de-toranzo-circular-route-through-the-cajigal-de-salcedillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conjunto Tumular de Quintana de Toranzo – circular route through the Cajigal de Salcedillo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.2 km path that explores historical sites and rural landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24642615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Piedrona de Villegar – Churrón de Borleña Waterfall loop from Santiurde de Toranzo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.7 km.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see on the trails?
A: Hikers can discover several natural highlights. The spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a popular destination. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4847953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintana IV Tumulus&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4847986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintana II Burial Mound&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Corvera De Toranzo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from lush mountains to river valleys, and the presence of natural waterfalls. The well-maintained trails and varied options for all ability levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or offer waterside views?
A: Yes, the Pas River is a significant natural feature of the region. Hiking along its banks offers peaceful walks and opportunities to observe local wildlife. The Vía Verde del Pas, a natural path following a former railway line, also provides an easy way to enjoy the scenery along the river.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Corvera De Toranzo?
A: Corvera De Toranzo is suitable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, making them ideal. Summer provides warmer weather for exploring, while winter can offer unique, quieter experiences, though some higher routes might require more preparation.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Corvera De Toranzo features cultural and historical points of interest. You can find traditional 'casonas' and emblazoned palaces, particularly in the Alceda Historical-Artistic Site. The Ethnographic Museum 'El Hombre y el Campo' in San Vicente de Toranzo also offers insights into local culture.

Q: How long are the typical hikes in Corvera De Toranzo?
A: Hikes in Corvera De Toranzo vary greatly in length. Shorter, easy routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996713975/churron-de-borlena-via-the-la-llana-stream-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña via the La Llana stream path&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Toranzo Park – Monte Cabaña Trail loop from San Vicente de Toranzo&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 4 hours, while longer routes might extend to 5 hours or more.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While many routes are easy to moderate, Corvera De Toranzo also offers more challenging options. The region's lush green mountains and areas within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park provide itineraries with greater elevation gain and longer distances, suitable for experienced hikers seeking a more demanding adventure.
tips:
This waterfall is located in Borleña (Corvera de Toranzo) and is formed by the Arroyo de la Llana, a tributary of the Pas River, which falls sliding from a large rock (sometimes with little water) but which reaches a beautiful pool at its feet that makes you want to put your feet in it.
The churrón is reached through a beautiful easy path, for all audiences and through a beautiful forest and that does not reach 2.5 km from the Bolera de Borleña.
Very close to the renowned Borleña churrón is this other waterfall, not so popular but worth visiting being so close.

faqs.question: Where does the Arroyo de la Llana River Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail begins near the village of Borleña. While specific parking details for this exact trailhead are not provided, it's generally accessible from the local area. Given its nature as a river trail, access points are typically well-known locally.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Arroyo de la Llana River Trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on trails like this. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's location and the presence of waterfalls, spring and autumn are often ideal. Spring offers lush greenery and full waterfalls, while autumn provides pleasant temperatures and potentially colorful foliage. Summer can be warm, but the river environment might offer some respite.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required for the Arroyo de la Llana River Trail itself. It appears to be a publicly accessible path.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Arroyo de la Llana River Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail follows a river, so you can expect varied terrain typical of a river path. This often includes sections alongside the water, potentially with some uneven ground, and possibly transitions through shaded areas. The overall difficulty is rated as easy.

faqs.question: What notable features or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is known for its beautiful waterfalls. You'll encounter the impressive Churrón de Borleña Waterfall and the scenic Churrón de Castillo. These are significant natural highlights of the route.

faqs.question: Is the Arroyo de la Llana River Trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, with an 'easy' difficulty rating and a duration of just over an hour and a half, this trail is very suitable for beginners and families looking for a pleasant outdoor experience. The river and waterfalls provide engaging scenery.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other official trails. You will be on parts of the "Senda fluvial del Arroyo de la Llana" for a significant portion, and also briefly on the "Camino de Villegar a Borleña".

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this easy, relatively short trail, comfortable walking shoes are essential. Depending on the weather, consider bringing water, a light jacket, and perhaps a camera to capture the waterfalls. Sun protection might also be useful.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the Arroyo de la Llana River Trail?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly named as 'viewpoints,' the trail's primary attractions are the waterfalls, particularly the Churrón de Borleña Waterfall and Churrón de Castillo, which offer picturesque natural vistas along the river.

faqs.question: Is the Arroyo de la Llana River Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: Based on the provided tour data, the Arroyo de la Llana River Trail is an out-and-back route, meaning you will return along the same path you took to reach the highlights.
